checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. All body text in the JSON matches the textual content found in the source document. The titles and image alt texts are expected AI-generated additions for accessibility.

Every piece of contact information was verified against the source PDF. The name (Bob Coats), email (Bob.Coats@osbm.nc.gov), and phone number ((919) 807-4781) are identical between the source and the output.

All textual sections present in the source are accounted for in the output. Both pages of the factsheet are represented. While specific content from complex graphics is omitted from the text (addressed in C5), the sections themselves are present.
The source document does not contain any traditional data tables. The information is presented in paragraphs and graphics.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The conversion failed to transcribe significant textual information contained within graphics on Page 2. Specifically, the 'Timeline' graphic at the top of Page 2 contains detailed project milestones for every year from 2015 to 2021 (e.g., 'Estimate on-the-ground workloads', 'Develop and award major contracts', 'Conduct end-to-end systems test', 'Deliver apportionment counts'). Additionally, the funnel graphic contains final outcome labels ('Correct addresses', 'Correct boundaries', 'Correct streets') not found in the body text. This information is entirely missing from the text representation, and the provided alt text is too generic to capture these details. This loss accounts for more than 25% of the unique information on Page 2.
checks.b_evidence_source: The timeline graphic contains: '2015: Estimate on-the-ground workloads...', '2016: Develop and award major contracts...', '2017: Put management infrastructure...', '2018: Conduct end-to-end systems test', '2021: Deliver apportionment counts', etc. The funnel graphic contains: 'Correct addresses', 'Correct boundaries', 'Correct streets'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output only provides generic alt text: 'A timeline graphic spanning from 2014 to 2021 highlighting key census activities.' and 'A funnel graphic illustrating the collection and processing of census data.' The specific text from within the images is missing.
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 2, Image elements at reading_order 25 and 26.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The transcribed text is highly accurate. Numbers, dates, and proper nouns (e.g., '$590 billion', '$1,623 per person', 'Participants Statistical Areas Program') match the source exactly.

checks.a_analysis: I have carefully reviewed both pages of the source PDF and the provided JSON output. The JSON contains all the main content blocks once (Representation, Funding, Planning sidebars, main body text, program descriptions, and contact information). There are no instances where a paragraph or section is repeated in the output. The images (logos, timeline, funnel) are also represented uniquely in their respective sections.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. Section 1 uses an H1 for the main title and H2 for the major sub-header ('Census Day...') and the secondary title ('2020 Census'). Section 2 uses an H1 for its main title. Sub-headings for specific programs like 'Boundary Annexation Survey' are treated as bolded text within paragraphs, which is a common and acceptable normalization of a flat visual hierarchy. There are no skipped levels (e.g., H1 to H3).

The source document does not contain any formal tables. The content is organized using text blocks, sidebars, and graphics, all of which have been appropriately converted into paragraphs or images. Therefore, no table corruption has occurred.

The JSON output maintains a logical reading order that follows the general flow of the document. On Page 1, the main text is prioritized before the sidebar items, which is standard for accessibility. On Page 2, the text descriptions of the programs are presented before the contact information and final graphics. Although graphics like the timeline (which is visually at the top) are placed at the end of the section, this is a common and acceptable reordering that ensures the primary informational text is delivered first.

The program descriptions on Page 2 (BAS, LUCA, PSAP) are formatted as bolded headers followed by descriptions within single paragraphs. While visually distinct in the source, they are not bulleted or numbered, and converting them as distinct paragraphs preserves their meaning and structure perfectly.

The contact information in the footer of Page 2 includes a valid email address ('Bob.Coats@osbm.nc.gov') and phone number ('(919) 807-4781'). Both are present in the JSON (element index 24) and match the source exactly. There are no broken or fabricated links injected into the document.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML version successfully transitions the source's multi-column and sidebar-based layout into a single-column flow suitable for web browsing. The sidebars from page 1 ('Representation', 'Funding', 'Planning') are placed logically within the content flow. The visual organization of the timeline and the funnel graphic on page 2 is maintained as images. A user can easily find all information in a logical sequence that mirrors the intent of the original document.

There are no traditional data tables with rows and columns in the source document. The statistical information and timeline are rendered as graphical elements or styled text blocks, which appear correctly in the HTML.

All meaningful images and graphics from the source PDF are present in the HTML. This includes the North Carolina map, the 2020 Census logo, the icons for population and household statistics, the timeline graphic, the funnel infographic, and the final 'Correct addresses' person graphic. The small branding logo from the top-left of page 1 is absent from the immediate top of the HTML, but its content (Census logo and NC map) is present later in the document, which is a standard accessibility practice for redundant decorative headers.

There is no genuine duplication of body content paragraphs or sections. While text strings like '330+ million people...' and 'Correct addresses, Correct boundaries, Correct streets' appear both within graphics and as text labels immediately below them, this is interpreted as a standard accessibility feature (text fallbacks/descriptions for complex visual layouts) rather than a content error. The repetition of headings in the Table of Contents and the body is expected design. No paragraphs or blocks of narrative text are duplicated.
